verses.latin_vulgate: Post hæc egressus ab Athenis, venit Corinthum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ ταῦτα χωρισθεὶς ὁ παῦλος ἐκ τῶν ἀθηνῶν ἦλθεν εἰς κόρινθον
verses.king_james: After these things Paul departed from Athens, and came to Corinth;

verses.latin_vulgate: et inveniens quemdam Judæum nomine Aquilam, Ponticum genere, qui nuper venerat ab Italia, et Priscillam uxorem ejus (eo quod præcepisset Claudius discedere omnes Judæos a Roma), accessit ad eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὑρών τινα ἰουδαῖον ὀνόματι ἀκύλαν ποντικὸν τῷ γένει προσφάτως ἐληλυθότα ἀπὸ τῆς ἰταλίας καὶ πρίσκιλλαν γυναῖκα αὐτοῦ διὰ τὸ διατεταχέναι κλαύδιον χωρίζεσθαι πάντας τοὺς ἰουδαίους ἐκ τῆς ῥώμης προσῆλθεν αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And found a certain Jew named Aquila, born in Pontus, lately come from Italy, with his wife Priscilla; (because that Claudius had commanded all Jews to depart from Rome:) and came unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quia ejusdem erat artis, manebat apud eos, et operabatur. (Erant autem scenofactoriæ artis.)
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διὰ τὸ ὁμότεχνον εἶναι ἔμενεν παρ᾽ αὐτοῖς καὶ εἰργάζετο ἦσαν γὰρ σκηνοποιοὶ τήν τέχνην
verses.king_james: And because he was of the same craft, he abode with them, and wrought: for by their occupation they were tentmakers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et disputabat in synagoga per omne sabbatum, interponens nomen Domini Jesu: suadebatque Judæis et Græcis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διελέγετο δὲ ἐν τῇ συναγωγῇ κατὰ πᾶν σάββατον ἔπειθέν τε ἰουδαίους καὶ ἕλληνας
verses.king_james: And he reasoned in the synagogue every sabbath, and persuaded the Jews and the Greeks.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum venissent autem de Macedonia Silas et Timotheus, instabat verbo Paulus, testificans Judæis esse Christum Jesum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς δὲ κατῆλθον ἀπὸ τῆς μακεδονίας ὅ τε σιλᾶς καὶ ὁ τιμόθεος συνείχετο τῷ πνεύματι ὁ παῦλος διαμαρτυρόμενος τοῖς ἰουδαίοις τὸν χριστόν ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: And when Silas and Timotheus were come from Macedonia, Paul was pressed in the spirit, and testified to the Jews *that* Jesus *was* Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Contradicentibus autem eis, et blasphemantibus, excutiens vestimenta sua, dixit ad eos: Sanguis vester super caput vestrum: mundus ego: ex hoc ad gentes vadam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀντιτασσομένων δὲ αὐτῶν καὶ βλασφημούντων ἐκτιναξάμενος τὰ ἱμάτια εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς τὸ αἷμα ὑμῶν ἐπὶ τὴν κεφαλὴν ὑμῶν καθαρὸς ἐγώ ἀπὸ τοῦ νῦν εἰς τὰ ἔθνη πορεύσομαι
verses.king_james: And when they opposed themselves, and blasphemed, he shook *his* raiment, and said unto them, Your blood *be* upon your own heads; I *am* clean: from henceforth I will go unto the Gentiles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et migrans inde, intravit in domum cujusdam, nomine Titi Justi, colentis Deum, cujus domus erat conjuncta synagogæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μεταβὰς ἐκεῖθεν ἦλθεν εἰς οἰκίαν τινὸς ὀνόματι ἰούστου σεβομένου τὸν θεόν οὗ ἡ οἰκία ἦν συνομοροῦσα τῇ συναγωγῇ
verses.king_james: And he departed thence, and entered into a certain *man's* house, named Justus, *one* that worshipped God, whose house joined hard to the synagogue.

verses.latin_vulgate: Crispus autem archisynagogus credidit Domino cum omni domo sua: et multi Corinthiorum audientes credebant, et baptizabantur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κρίσπος δὲ ὁ ἀρχισυνάγωγος ἐπίστευσεν τῷ κυρίῳ σὺν ὅλῳ τῷ οἴκῳ αὐτοῦ καὶ πολλοὶ τῶν κορινθίων ἀκούοντες ἐπίστευον καὶ ἐβαπτίζοντο
verses.king_james: And Crispus, the chief ruler of the synagogue, believed on the Lord with all his house; and many of the Corinthians hearing believed, and were baptized.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem Dominus nocte per visionem Paulo: Noli timere, sed loquere, et ne taceas:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ ὁ κύριος δι᾽ ὁράματος ἐν νυκτὶ τῷ παύλῳ μὴ φοβοῦ ἀλλὰ λάλει καὶ μὴ σιωπήσῃς
verses.king_james: Then spake the Lord to Paul in the night by a vision, Be not afraid, but speak, and hold not thy peace:

verses.latin_vulgate: propter quod ego sum tecum, et nemo apponetur tibi ut noceat te: quoniam populus est mihi multus in hac civitate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διότι ἐγώ εἰμι μετὰ σοῦ καὶ οὐδεὶς ἐπιθήσεταί σοι τοῦ κακῶσαί σε διότι λαός ἐστίν μοι πολὺς ἐν τῇ πόλει ταύτῃ
verses.king_james: For I am with thee, and no man shall set on thee to hurt thee: for I have much people in this city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sedit autem ibi annum et sex menses, docens apud eos verbum Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκάθισεν τε ἐνιαυτὸν καὶ μῆνας ἓξ διδάσκων ἐν αὐτοῖς τὸν λόγον τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And he continued *there* a year and six months, teaching the word of God among them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gallione autem proconsule Achaiæ, insurrexerunt uno animo Judæi in Paulum, et adduxerunt eum ad tribunal,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γαλλίωνος δὲ ἀνθυπατεύοντος τῆς ἀχαΐας κατεπέστησαν ὁμοθυμαδὸν οἱ ἰουδαῖοι τῷ παύλῳ καὶ ἤγαγον αὐτὸν ἐπὶ τὸ βῆμα
verses.king_james: And when Gallio was the deputy of Achaia, the Jews made insurrection with one accord against Paul, and brought him to the judgment seat,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicentes: Quia contra legem hic persuadet hominibus colere Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγοντες ὅτι παρὰ τὸν νόμον οὗτος ἀναπείθει τοὺς ἀνθρώπους σέβεσθαι τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: Saying, This *fellow* persuadeth men to worship God contrary to the law.

verses.latin_vulgate: Incipiente autem Paulo aperire os, dixit Gallio ad Judæos: Si quidem esset iniquum aliquid aut facinus pessimum, o viri Judæi, recte vos sustinerem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μέλλοντος δὲ τοῦ παύλου ἀνοίγειν τὸ στόμα εἶπεν ὁ γαλλίων πρὸς τοὺς ἰουδαίους εἰ μὲν οὖν ἦν ἀδίκημά τι ἢ ῥᾳδιούργημα πονηρόν ὦ ἰουδαῖοι κατὰ λόγον ἂν ἠνεσχόμην ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And when Paul was now about to open *his* mouth, Gallio said unto the Jews, If it were a matter of wrong or wicked lewdness, O *ye* Jews, reason would that I should bear with you:

verses.latin_vulgate: Si vero quæstiones sunt de verbo, et nominibus, et lege vestra, vos ipsi videritis: judex ego horum nolo esse.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ ζήτημά ἐστιν περὶ λόγου καὶ ὀνομάτων καὶ νόμου τοῦ καθ᾽ ὑμᾶς ὄψεσθε αὐτοί κριτὴς γὰρ ἐγὼ τούτων οὐ βούλομαι εἶναι
verses.king_james: But if it be a question of words and names, and *of* your law, look ye *to it*; for I will be no judge of such *matters*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et minavit eos a tribunali.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπήλασεν αὐτοὺς ἀπὸ τοῦ βήματος
verses.king_james: And he drave them from the judgment seat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Apprehendentes autem omnes Sosthenem principem synagogæ, percutiebant eum ante tribunal: et nihil eorum Gallioni curæ erat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπιλαβόμενοι δὲ πάντες οἱ ἕλληνες σωσθένην τὸν ἀρχισυνάγωγον ἔτυπτον ἔμπροσθεν τοῦ βήματος καὶ οὐδὲν τούτων τῷ γαλλίωνι ἔμελεν
verses.king_james: Then all the Greeks took Sosthenes, the chief ruler of the synagogue, and beat *him* before the judgment seat. And Gallio cared for none of those things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Paulus vero cum adhuc sustinuisset dies multos fratribus valefaciens, navigavit in Syriam (et cum eo Priscilla et Aquila), qui sibi totonderat in Cenchris caput: habebat enim votum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ παῦλος ἔτι προσμείνας ἡμέρας ἱκανὰς τοῖς ἀδελφοῖς ἀποταξάμενος ἐξέπλει εἰς τὴν συρίαν καὶ σὺν αὐτῷ πρίσκιλλα καὶ ἀκύλας κειράμενος τὴν κεφαλήν ἐν κεγχρεαῖς εἶχεν γὰρ εὐχήν
verses.king_james: And Paul *after this* tarried *there* yet a good while, and then took his leave of the brethren, and sailed thence into Syria, and with him Priscilla and Aquila; having shorn *his* head in Cenchrea: for he had a vow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Devenitque Ephesum, et illos ibi reliquit. Ipse vero ingressus synagogam, disputabat cum Judæis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατήντησεν δὲ εἰς ἔφεσον κἀκείνους κατέλιπεν αὐτοῦ αὐτὸς δὲ εἰσελθὼν εἰς τὴν συναγωγὴν διελέχθη τοῖς ἰουδαίοις
verses.king_james: And he came to Ephesus, and left them there: but he himself entered into the synagogue, and reasoned with the Jews.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rogantibus autem eis ut ampliori tempore maneret, non consensit,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐρωτώντων δὲ αὐτῶν ἐπὶ πλείονα χρόνον μεῖναι παρ᾽ αὐτοῖς, οὐκ ἐπένευσεν
verses.king_james: When they desired *him* to tarry longer time with them, he consented not;

verses.latin_vulgate: sed valefaciens, et dicens: Iterum revertar ad vos, Deo volente: profectus est ab Epheso.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ἀπετάξατο αὐτοῖς, εἰπών δεῖ με πάντως τὴν ἑορτὴν τὴν ἐρχομενην ποιῆσαι εἰς ἰεροσόλυμα, πάλιν δὲ ἀνακάμψω πρὸς ὑμᾶς τοῦ θεοῦ θέλοντος καὶ ἀνήχθη ἀπὸ τῆς ἐφέσου
verses.king_james: But bade them farewell, saying, I must by all means keep this feast that cometh in Jerusalem: but I will return again unto you, if God will. And he sailed from Ephesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et descendens Cæsaream, ascendit, et salutavit ecclesiam, et descendit Antiochiam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ κατελθὼν εἰς καισάρειαν ἀναβὰς καὶ ἀσπασάμενος τὴν ἐκκλησίαν κατέβη εἰς ἀντιόχειαν
verses.king_james: And when he had landed at Caesarea, and gone up, and saluted the church, he went down to Antioch.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et facto ibi aliquanto tempore profectus est, perambulans ex ordine Galaticam regionem, et Phrygiam, confirmans omnes discipulos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ποιήσας χρόνον τινὰ ἐξῆλθεν διερχόμενος καθεξῆς τὴν γαλατικὴν χώραν καὶ φρυγίαν ἐπιστηρίζων πάντας τοὺς μαθητάς
verses.king_james: And after he had spent some time *there*, he departed, and went over *all* he country of Galatia and Phrygia in order, strengthening all the disciples.

verses.latin_vulgate: Judæus autem quidam, Apollo nomine, Alexandrinus genere, vir eloquens, devenit Ephesum, potens in scripturis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰουδαῖος δέ τις ἀπολλῶς ὀνόματι ἀλεξανδρεὺς τῷ γένει ἀνὴρ λόγιος κατήντησεν εἰς ἔφεσον δυνατὸς ὢν ἐν ταῖς γραφαῖς
verses.king_james: And a certain Jew named Apollos, born at Alexandria, an eloquent man, *and* mighty in the scriptures, came to Ephesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic erat edoctus viam Domini: et fervens spiritu loquebatur, et docebat diligenter ea quæ sunt Jesu, sciens tantum baptisma Joannis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτος ἦν κατηχημένος τὴν ὁδὸν τοῦ κυρίου καὶ ζέων τῷ πνεύματι ἐλάλει καὶ ἐδίδασκεν ἀκριβῶς τὰ περὶ τοῦ κυρίου, ἐπιστάμενος μόνον τὸ βάπτισμα ἰωάννου
verses.king_james: This man was instructed in the way of the Lord; and being fervent in the spirit, he spake and taught diligently the things of the Lord, knowing only the baptism of John.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic ergo cœpit fiducialiter agere in synagoga. Quem cum audissent Priscilla et Aquila, assumpserunt eum, et diligentius exposuerunt ei viam Domini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτός τε ἤρξατο παῤῥησιάζεσθαι ἐν τῇ συναγωγῇ ἀκούσαντες δὲ αὐτοῦ ἀκύλας καὶ πρίσκιλλα προσελάβοντο αὐτὸν καὶ ἀκριβέστερον αὐτῷ ἐξέθεντο τὴν τοῦ θεοῦ ὁδὸν
verses.king_james: And he began to speak boldly in the synagogue: whom when Aquila and Priscilla had heard, they took him unto *them*, and expounded unto him the way of God more perfectly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem vellet ire Achaiam, exhortati fratres, scripserunt discipulis ut susciperent eum. Qui cum venisset, contulit multum his qui crediderant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βουλομένου δὲ αὐτοῦ διελθεῖν εἰς τὴν ἀχαΐαν προτρεψάμενοι οἱ ἀδελφοὶ ἔγραψαν τοῖς μαθηταῖς ἀποδέξασθαι αὐτόν ὃς παραγενόμενος συνεβάλετο πολὺ τοῖς πεπιστευκόσιν διὰ τῆς χάριτος
verses.king_james: And when he was disposed to pass into Achaia, the brethren wrote, exhorting the disciples to receive him: who, when he was come, helped them much which had believed through grace:

verses.latin_vulgate: Vehementer enim Judæos revincebat publice, ostendens per Scripturas esse Christum Jesum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐτόνως γὰρ τοῖς ἰουδαίοις διακατηλέγχετο δημοσίᾳ ἐπιδεικνὺς διὰ τῶν γραφῶν εἶναι τὸν χριστὸν ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: For he mightily convinced the Jews, *and that* publickly, shewing by the scriptures that Jesus was Christ.
